Redick highlighted costly turnovers, especially in the second quarter.
The team now faces a must‑win situation at the Toyota Center.

Despite the dip, bookmakers still list the Lakers as the series favorite.
The development underscores the impact of the Rockets' comeback on betting markets.

articles.title: LeBron Calls Out Jabari Smith After Lakers’ Loss to Rockets
articles.original_title: 'Too Old for That S--t,' LeBron Reacts to Jabari Smith's Diss in Video After Lakers Loss to Rockets - Bleacher Report

key_points: Lakers fell 6 points to the Rockets, 99‑93.
Jabari Smith posted a diss video aimed at LeBron.
LeBron replied publicly, dismissing the jab as immature.

Coaching staff urged to secure additional scoring contributors.

articles.title: Rockets Edge Lakers 99‑93 in Defensive Battle
articles.original_title: Houston Rockets 99 - Los Angeles Lakers 93: Final score, results, recap, box score, stats - Yahoo Sports
articles.summary: Houston secured a 99‑93 victory over Los Angeles, with strong defensive play limiting the Lakers to under 100 points. The box score showed balanced scoring from the Rockets and a sub‑par shooting night for the Lakers’ stars.
key_points: Final score: Rockets 99, Lakers 93.
Lakers shot below their season average, missing key shots in the fourth quarter.
Houston’s defense forced 12 turnovers and held the Lakers to 38% from the field.

articles.title: Rockets Edge Lakers to Force Game 6

key_points: Rockets secured a narrow win after a fourth‑quarter comeback.
Series score narrowed to Lakers 3, Rockets 2.
Los Angeles now faces elimination in Game 6.

articles.title: Cavaliers Capitalize on Schroder’s Fourth‑Quarter Burst to Lead Series
articles.original_title: Cavs let Schroder cook in 4th, regain series contr...
articles.summary: Dennis Schroder, limited in the first four games, exploded for 11 of his 19 points in the fourth quarter, helping Cleveland defeat the Lakers 125‑120. The win gave the Cavs a 3‑2 edge in the Eastern Conference semifinals.
key_points: Schroder’s 11 fourth‑quarter points sparked the comeback.
Cavaliers win 125‑120, taking a 3‑2 series lead.
Lakers now trail and must win the next two games to avoid elimination.

key_points: Durant ruled out for Game 6 because of an ankle injury.

Houston must find alternative scoring options against the Lakers.

articles.title: John Wall Named President of Basketball Operations at Howard University
articles.original_title: Wall becomes Howard's prez of basketball ops
articles.summary: Five‑time NBA All‑Star John Wall has accepted the role of president of basketball operations at Howard University, returning to Washington, D.C., to help lead the nation’s top‑ranked HBCU basketball program. The hire signals Howard’s commitment to building a competitive collegiate program.
key_points: Wall will oversee all basketball‑related decisions at Howard.
His hiring aligns with Howard’s status as the top‑ranked HBCU in basketball.
Wall returns to his hometown to contribute to college athletics.

articles.title: Cade Cunningham Drops Franchise Playoff‑Record 45 as Pistons Edge Magic
articles.original_title: Cade's 45 do trick vs. Magic as Pistons stay alive...
articles.summary: Cade Cunningham scored a franchise‑record 45 points, including a clutch step‑back jumper with 32 seconds left, to lead the Detroit Pistons past the Orlando Magic and keep their playoff hopes alive. The performance helped Detroit stave off elimination in the series.
key_points: Cunningham’s 45 points set a new Pistons playoff scoring record.
His late step‑back jumper secured the win in the final minute.
The victory prevents Detroit’s elimination and forces a decisive game.

His absence could impact the Raptors’ chances in the remaining games.

He points out differences between the eras and playing styles.
James refrains from declaring a final GOAT verdict.

key_points: LeBron surpassed Michael Jordan in a cumulative career statistic (e.g., points, assists, or minutes).
The new record underscores his longevity and consistent high‑level performance.
